maturity_policy: candidate-only; execution is required before fixture-verified or data-verified promotion

cases.title: KinneyH cell annotation composition negative-control figure
cases.purpose: Use a real 56,179-cell annotation table to detect percentage, axis-label and label-collision failures, then plan scientifically bounded sample-level composition figures; donor-aware inference requires a separately verified donor-sample mapping.

availability.evidence_scope: One CSV was opened read-only; header and 56,179 data rows across five sample IDs were confirmed. Existing plots were not accepted as reference truth.

data_contract.role: cell-level annotations, sample IDs and QC covariates
data_contract.expected_shape: 56,179 rows x 18 columns; five sample IDs

statistical_contract.biological_replicate: not established; sample_id is a descriptive grouping variable and cannot be assumed to equal donor_id
statistical_contract.inference_unit: unresolved until donor-sample mapping and independence are verified; no cell-level or sample-level inferential test
statistical_contract.estimand: per-sample cell-type composition; descriptive only because no case-control contrast is registered
statistical_contract.pseudoreplication_guard: Aggregate counts and proportions by sample before any uncertainty or group comparison; never treat 56,179 cells as independent donors.
statistical_contract.missingness_policy: Report missing sample_id/cell_type values and exclude only with an explicit count ledger.

stages.purpose: Validate columns, row count, sample count, missingness and category levels.
Build sample-by-cell-type counts and within-sample proportions with exact denominator checks.
Plan stacked bars and sample-level distribution alternatives without changing the descriptive estimand.
Review percent sums, axis semantics, color discrimination, labels and unsupported claims.

optional_branches: donor-level dot or interval plot only after donor-sample mapping, donor independence and a valid grouping variable are verified

dependencies.name: pandas
dependencies.version_policy: freeze probed compatible version

dependencies.name: matplotlib
dependencies.version_policy: freeze probed compatible version

dependencies.name: seaborn
dependencies.version_policy: freeze probed compatible version

validation: row and sample counts are recorded
each sample proportion sums to 1 within tolerance
variants preserve the same descriptive estimand

acceptance_criteria: Every sample's stacked composition sums to 100% within numerical tolerance.
Axes and legends identify percent, sample and cell type without contradictory labels.
No inferential or condition-effect claim is made from cell-level counts.
Figure notes explicitly document the known over-100%, wrong-axis and label-overlap negative controls.

governance.required_action: Resolve dataset redistribution and reuse terms before packaging any data; retain the verified GSE185948/PMID 36310237 citation.

blockers.description: The registered table has five samples but no validated inferential contrast.
blockers.resolution: Use descriptive composition only or register condition metadata and an estimand in a new WorkflowInstance.

blockers.description: Prior plots include stacks above 100%, an incorrect axis label and overlapping pie labels.
blockers.resolution: Treat them as negative controls, recompute denominators and complete native visual review.

cases.claim_ceiling: Supports teaching and auditing descriptive sample-level composition visualization; it cannot establish disease-associated composition changes.
cases.next_allowed_action: Compile a reproduce-figure plan; execution still requires explicit run authorization and a frozen task environment.

cases.title: GSE185948 five-donor healthy kidney 10x snRNA-seq
cases.purpose: Exercise nuclear-count import, donor-preserving nucleus-aware QC, integration choice, clustering and annotation while preventing nucleus-level pseudoreplication and forced labels.

availability.evidence_scope: Five readable 10x snRNA-seq HDF5 matrices were hashed read-only; 36,601 features and 56,728 total filtered nucleus barcodes were confirmed. GSE185948 metadata were frozen separately; no biological analysis was run.

data_contract.role: raw candidate input: five filtered 10x single-nucleus feature-barcode matrices

statistical_contract.estimand: descriptive healthy-kidney nuclear atlas and per-donor QC; all five donors are controls, so no condition contrast is available
statistical_contract.pseudoreplication_guard: Use pseudobulk or donor-level models for any later differential analysis and preserve donor IDs through integration.
statistical_contract.missingness_policy: Record feature/nucleus filtering and missing metadata by donor; never drop a donor silently.

stages.purpose: Read five matrices separately and validate features, nucleus barcodes, donor mapping, public metadata and integer counts.

Plan per-donor nucleus-aware thresholds from observed UMI, feature and complexity distributions; record mitochondrial, ribosomal and intronic fractions diagnostically without imposing canned whole-cell mitochondrial cutoffs; plan doublet and ambient-RNA checks with a retained-nucleus ledger.
Select normalization and integration only after quantifying donor/batch structure; do not substitute ComBat for Harmony.
Cluster nuclei across resolutions, annotate with marker and reference evidence, and preserve unknown/ambiguous labels.
Review per-donor nuclear composition, mixing, label confidence and claim boundaries.

optional_branches: ambient RNA correction when contamination is demonstrated
donor-aware differential testing only after a valid contrast is added

dependencies.name: scanpy
dependencies.version_policy: freeze probed compatible version

dependencies.name: anndata
dependencies.version_policy: freeze probed compatible version

dependencies.name: scrublet
dependencies.version_policy: resolve only after run authorization

dependencies.name: harmonypy
dependencies.version_policy: optional; install only if Harmony is methodologically selected

validation: donor IDs are unique and nonmissing
thresholds are distribution-derived and reported per donor
no canned whole-cell mitochondrial threshold is imposed
doublet and ambient decisions are justified
biological signal and donor mixing diagnostics reviewed

acceptance_criteria: All five input hashes, matrix dimensions and donor mappings reconcile.
QC thresholds are nucleus-aware, donor-specific and distribution-derived; no canned whole-cell mitochondrial threshold is used.
Doublet and ambient-RNA decisions are donor-aware and fully logged.
The reported integration method is the method actually executed and has diagnostic evidence.
Annotation retains ambiguous/unknown nuclei and reports marker/reference evidence.
Any inferential test uses donor-level biological replication and FDR control.

governance.required_action: Keep the hash-bound GSE185948 metadata and verified citation fixed; resolve dataset redistribution and reuse terms before packaging any input data.

blockers.description: Public metadata and citation are verified, but dataset redistribution and reuse terms remain pending.
blockers.resolution: Analyze local read-only inputs only after run authorization; resolve terms before packaging or redistributing data.

blockers.description: Scrublet and harmonypy are not installed in the probed base environment.
blockers.resolution: After explicit run authorization, resolve only the selected methods in a new task environment.

cases.claim_ceiling: Supports metadata-frozen data profiling and workflow planning for a five-donor healthy-kidney nuclear atlas; it does not support case-control inference, clinical associations or validated nucleus labels.
cases.next_allowed_action: Compile a deterministic plan from the hash-bound public metadata; do not execute, install dependencies or reuse scientifically problematic legacy outputs as validation truth without explicit run authorization.

cases.title: Spotiphy mouse-brain ST, scRNA reference, histology and Visium HD candidate
cases.purpose: Exercise coordinate/image QC, reference harmonization, deconvolution/decomposition and spatial interpretation with explicit spot-versus-section units.

availability.evidence_scope: All four artifacts were hashed read-only; ST shape 685 x 32,285 and scRNA-reference shape 10,500 x 33,526 were confirmed through HDF5 structure only.

data_contract.expected_shape: native image dimensions to be registered during data profile

data_contract.role: optional Visium HD segmentation tutorial input
data_contract.expected_shape: archive content requires read-only manifest before extraction

statistical_contract.measurement_unit: spot/bin/cell and pixel, depending on branch
statistical_contract.inference_unit: section/animal; the candidate appears to contain one ST section and is therefore tutorial-descriptive
statistical_contract.estimand: spot-level cell-type abundance and spatial reconstruction for one tutorial section
statistical_contract.pseudoreplication_guard: Do not treat spots or pixels as independent animals and do not perform population inference from one section.
statistical_contract.missingness_policy: Report missing genes across ST/reference and unmapped/out-of-tissue coordinates; never impute spatial coordinates.

stages.purpose: Validate counts, coordinates, image transform, platform and section identity.
Align feature identifiers and assess reference labels without leaking spatial outputs into reference training.
Plan Spotiphy abundance/decomposition with frozen parameters and uncertainty outputs.
Overlay results on histology and review coordinates, normalization, color scales and claim limits.

optional_branches: Visium HD archive manifest and segmentation
spatial neighborhood analysis after section-level replication exists

dependencies.name: scanpy
dependencies.version_policy: freeze probed compatible version

dependencies.name: anndata
dependencies.version_policy: freeze probed compatible version

dependencies.name: spotiphy
dependencies.version_policy: pin audited local repository commit after license/citation verification

dependencies.name: squidpy
dependencies.version_policy: optional spatial QA dependency resolved only after run authorization

acceptance_criteria: Spatial coordinates and histology transforms are explicitly validated before modeling.
ST/reference feature overlap, label provenance and count layers are documented.
Model convergence, uncertainty and abundance constraints are reviewed.
Figures distinguish spot/bin/cell and do not imply replicated-animal inference.
Visium HD remains an optional branch until archive contents and segmentation provenance are audited.

governance.required_action: Record the exact repository commit, primary paper citation, Zenodo dataset record and data-specific reuse terms.

blockers.description: Apache-2.0 was verified for the code repository, but the tutorial data license and exact citation are not frozen.
blockers.resolution: Audit the referenced Zenodo record and primary paper before execution or packaging.

blockers.description: Spotiphy is only present as local source and squidpy is absent from the probed environment.
blockers.resolution: After run authorization, create a new compatible lock; do not reuse the incompatible base environment.

cases.claim_ceiling: Supports planning and a single-section tutorial of spatial decomposition; it cannot establish population-level spatial differences or biological causality.
cases.next_allowed_action: Complete license/citation and environment compatibility audits, then compile a plan without installing packages.

cases.title: Bioconductor airway paired dexamethasone bulk RNA-seq candidate
cases.purpose: Provide a compact paired-design teaching case for count QC, DESeq2 modeling, effect sizes and FDR with cell line as the biological block.

availability.evidence_scope: No airway dataset file was found in the audited Documents, Desktop or MultiOmics_Code_Collection roots; the airway package is not installed in R 4.5.3.

data_contract.role: raw gene-by-sample counts and sample metadata
data_contract.expected_shape: 8 samples from 4 cell lines, paired untreated versus dexamethasone

statistical_contract.estimand: average dexamethasone-associated log2 fold change adjusted for cell line
statistical_contract.pseudoreplication_guard: Use a paired design such as ~ cell_line + dex; do not treat the eight libraries as unpaired independent donors.
statistical_contract.missingness_policy: Reject missing pair or treatment labels; report filtered low-count genes rather than treating zeros as missing values.

stages.purpose: After authorization, restore the pinned package dataset and freeze its hash, metadata and citation.
Validate integer counts, paired design, library sizes, sample similarity and outliers.
Fit paired DESeq2 model, report effect sizes, uncertainty and BH FDR.
Create diagnostic/result figures and state what the paired experiment can and cannot establish.

optional_branches: limma-voom or edgeR comparison using the same paired estimand
pathway enrichment with universe and FDR registered

dependencies.name: airway
dependencies.version_policy: pin to Bioconductor release compatible with R 4.5.3

dependencies.name: SummarizedExperiment
dependencies.version_policy: freeze probed compatible version

dependencies.name: DESeq2
dependencies.version_policy: resolve after run authorization

dependencies.name: edgeR
dependencies.version_policy: optional comparison; freeze only if selected

dependencies.name: limma
dependencies.version_policy: optional comparison; freeze only if selected

acceptance_criteria: The cached input has verified package, dataset, citation and hash provenance.
The paired cell-line design is full rank and matches the declared estimand.
Count-scale assumptions, filtering and size-factor normalization are documented.
Results include log2 fold change, uncertainty and BH-adjusted P values.
Alternative engines are compared only under the same input and paired design.

governance.required_action: Resolve the exact Bioconductor release, package license, source experiment citation and dataset hashes.

blockers.description: The airway package dataset is not present locally.
blockers.resolution: After explicit run authorization, restore a pinned Bioconductor environment and record immutable input hashes.

blockers.description: DESeq2 is not installed in the probed R environment.
blockers.resolution: Provision it only in a new task-scoped renv environment after authorization.

cases.claim_ceiling: Currently supports a paired bulk-RNA method plan only; no input has been cached and no differential result exists.
cases.next_allowed_action: Keep at plan status until the user authorizes a task-local restore and data acquisition.

cases.title: DEP UbiLength 12-sample LFQ proteomics candidate
cases.purpose: Exercise protein-level filtering, normalization, missingness-mechanism review, differential abundance and sensitivity analysis without hiding imputation assumptions.

availability.evidence_scope: No UbiLength data file was found in the audited Documents, Desktop or MultiOmics_Code_Collection roots; DEP, QFeatures and MSstats are not installed in R 4.5.3.

data_contract.role: protein-by-sample LFQ matrix plus experimental design
data_contract.expected_shape: 3,006 proteins x 12 LFQ samples; four conditions with three replicates each according to the official DEP manual, still to be verified from the materialized colData

statistical_contract.biological_replicate: independent sample; three replicates within each of four conditions according to the official DEP manual
statistical_contract.estimand: condition-associated log2 protein abundance difference for a predeclared contrast
statistical_contract.pseudoreplication_guard: Do not count peptides, proteins or technical channels as independent biological replicates.
statistical_contract.missingness_policy: Diagnose MCAR/MAR/MNAR plausibility by condition and abundance; compare no-imputation and justified imputation strategies, never silently replace missing LFQ values.

stages.purpose: After authorization, pin DEP dataset version, design, license, citation and input hashes.
Filter contaminants/reverse hits, verify log scale, replicate structure and missingness patterns.
Evaluate normalization, missingness strategy and differential model with explicit contrast and FDR.
Review diagnostics, effect-size figures and claims under missingness sensitivity.

optional_branches: QFeatures aggregation when peptide/PSM inputs are available
DEP versus limma or MSstats comparison only when inputs and estimands are compatible

dependencies.name: DEP
dependencies.version_policy: pin to Bioconductor release compatible with R 4.5.3

dependencies.name: QFeatures
dependencies.version_policy: optional aggregation layer resolved only if required

dependencies.name: limma
dependencies.version_policy: freeze probed compatible version

dependencies.name: MSstats
dependencies.version_policy: alternative method only; never automatic fallback

acceptance_criteria: The exact UbiLength version, 3,006 x 12 dimensions and experimental design are independently confirmed.
Contaminant, reverse-hit, filtering and scale decisions are traceable.
Missingness mechanism and imputation are explicit and sensitivity-tested.
Differential results report contrast, effect size, uncertainty and FDR.
DEP, QFeatures and MSstats are not treated as interchangeable methods.

governance.required_action: Verify the exact DEP/UbiLength package version, human cell-line condition labels, package/data license and materialized hashes before acquisition.

blockers.description: The UbiLength dataset is not available locally.
blockers.resolution: After explicit run authorization, restore the pinned package dataset and hash its materialized inputs.

blockers.description: DEP, QFeatures and MSstats are absent from the probed R environment.
blockers.resolution: Provision only the selected stack in a new task-scoped renv environment.

blockers.description: The official manual documents four conditions with three replicates, but the 12-row experimental design has not been materialized and hash-verified locally.
blockers.resolution: Freeze colData and the contrast before fitting any model.

cases.claim_ceiling: Currently supports proteomics workflow planning and negative tests for missingness misuse; no protein-level result has been generated.
cases.next_allowed_action: Remain in plan mode until dataset acquisition and environment provisioning are explicitly authorized.

cases.title: CLL four-view incomplete multi-omics MOFA candidate
cases.purpose: Exercise patient alignment, view-specific missingness, scale audit, latent-factor modeling and stability review without silent complete-case selection.

availability.evidence_scope: HDF5 structure was inspected read-only: 200 union patients; mRNA 136 x 5,000, Methylation 196 x 4,248, Mutations 200 x 69 and Drugs 184 x 310.

data_contract.role: multi-view patient matrix with nonuniform view coverage
data_contract.expected_shape: 200 union patients; views: mRNA 136x5000, Methylation 196x4248, Mutations 200x69, Drugs 184x310

statistical_contract.estimand: latent multi-view factors and their stable associations with registered patient-level covariates
statistical_contract.pseudoreplication_guard: Join views by patient identifiers and never count four views as four independent subjects.
statistical_contract.missingness_policy: Preserve view-specific missingness, quantify coverage and avoid silent complete-case restriction; any filtering or likelihood assumption requires sensitivity analysis.

stages.purpose: Validate patient maps, view dimensions, feature IDs, scales and missingness patterns.
Choose view-specific transformations, feature filters and missing-data representation without data leakage.
Plan MOFA/MOFA-FLEX factor model with seeds, likelihoods, convergence and held-out or repeated-seed stability checks.
Review view contributions, feature weights, patient scores, stability and association claim limits.

optional_branches: MOFA2 versus MOFA-FLEX as alternative methods with explicit prior differences
downstream survival or drug association only after endpoints and leakage-safe validation are registered

dependencies.name: h5py
dependencies.version_policy: freeze probed compatible version for structural audit

dependencies.name: mudata
dependencies.version_policy: resolve after run authorization

dependencies.name: mofapy2
dependencies.version_policy: MOFA2 engine candidate; pin exact version after compatibility test

dependencies.name: mofaflex
dependencies.version_policy: alternative method; pin audited repository commit

acceptance_criteria: The 200-patient union and all four view dimensions and patient maps reconcile.
View-specific scales, likelihoods, filtering and missingness policies are explicit.
No complete-case restriction or feature selection occurs silently.
Convergence and repeated-seed or equivalent factor stability are reviewed.
Factor interpretation distinguishes exploratory association from prediction or causation.

governance.required_action: Trace the embedded CLL dataset to its original study, confirm data reuse terms, patient variables and required citations.

blockers.description: The repository license is BSD-3-Clause, but the underlying CLL dataset provenance and reuse terms are not frozen.
blockers.resolution: Trace and record the original dataset citation/license before packaging or analysis.

blockers.description: mudata and mofapy2 are absent; mofaflex exists only as local source.
blockers.resolution: After explicit run authorization, choose MOFA2 or MOFA-FLEX methodologically and create a new locked environment.

cases.claim_ceiling: Supports patient-alignment, missing-view and factor-model planning; no latent factor is currently fitted or biologically validated.
cases.next_allowed_action: Complete provenance and method-choice review, then compile a plan without installing the modeling stack.

cases.title: ROGUE tutorial source-flow negative-control methodology audit
cases.purpose: Teach method/code review by detecting randomized pseudo-samples, cell-level pseudoreplication, installer contamination and unsupported purity claims in a preserved source flow.

availability.evidence_scope: SourceFlowBundle flow-5bc6b8a1bc6fbcd4de4a was reviewed statically; the source randomly assigns cells to A/B/C/D pseudo-samples and includes installer/environment commands.

data_contract.role: article, ordered R code and code-figure lineage for methodology review
data_contract.expected_shape: one article, one 10,116-byte R script and nine referenced images in SourceFlowBundle flow-5bc6b8a1bc6fbcd4de4a

statistical_contract.measurement_unit: cell for the underlying example; code/claim for this audit
statistical_contract.biological_replicate: none in the randomized A/B/C/D construction
statistical_contract.inference_unit: not defined; synthetic cell groups cannot represent biological samples
statistical_contract.pseudoreplication_guard: Reject any sample-level comparison derived from randomly assigning individual cells to pseudo-samples; require real donor/sample IDs.
statistical_contract.missingness_policy: Not applicable to the methodology audit; any future ROGUE analysis must report cells excluded and undefined cell-type/sample combinations.

stages.purpose: Verify article/code hashes, order, packages, installer calls and code-image references.
Separate ROGUE cluster-purity use from invalid randomized sample comparisons and identify required biological replication.
Map code to figure semantics and record what a ROGUE boxplot can and cannot establish.

optional_branches: derive a clean AnalysisRecipe only after real sample IDs and a legal fixture are supplied
native figure review without promoting bundle execution maturity

dependencies.name: ROGUE
dependencies.version_policy: declaration only; official repository and commit must be whitelisted before any run

dependencies.name: SCP
dependencies.version_policy: source tutorial dependency; not required for static explain mode

dependencies.name: Seurat
dependencies.version_policy: static review does not depend on runtime version

dependencies.name: ggplot2
dependencies.version_policy: static review does not depend on runtime version

validation: installer calls and random grouping are recorded
visible values separated from biological claims

acceptance_criteria: The article and code remain hash-linked to SourceFlowBundle flow-5bc6b8a1bc6fbcd4de4a.
Random A/B/C/D cell grouping is identified as invalid biological replication.
Installer commands are excluded from any derived AnalysisRecipe.
ROGUE is described as a cluster-purity/heterogeneity metric, not proof of cell identity or condition effect.
Explain-mode review does not claim that the source code or figures were executed or reproduced.

governance.required_action: Keep article/code/images private; verify the primary ROGUE paper, package repository and article citation before publishing only a derived method summary.

blockers.description: Cells are randomly assigned to A/B/C/D and then treated as samples.
blockers.resolution: Replace with real donor/sample IDs; otherwise restrict use to a negative methodology example.

blockers.description: The source script includes pak installation and environment-specific commands.
blockers.resolution: Keep them in source provenance but remove them from any AnalysisRecipe; EnvironmentManager owns installation.

blockers.description: The extracted article and its images are private local corpus material with unresolved redistribution rights.
blockers.resolution: Do not include raw content in the public core; publish only lawful derived metadata and source identifiers.

cases.claim_ceiling: Supports static methodology critique and a negative-control MethodCard; it does not support a valid ROGUE biological result or code reproducibility claim.
cases.next_allowed_action: Complete explain-mode method and claim review; execution requires a separate legal fixture, real sample IDs and explicit run authorization.
